reviews.title: Cheap Buys Cheap
reviews.text: Not sure what I expected for so little, so shame on me. I'll tell you these made my 9-year-old's ears hurt. I'm not sure how they could cancel any noise, as there is no buffer. I tried the ones she put on and they are very uncomfortable. Returning.

reviews.title: Great product and great price.
reviews.text: We bought a pack of these for my son because he goes through headphones crazy fast; since we bought these, he's still on the first pair. We will be buying more in the future if we have to.

reviews.title: They are priced low enough that a defective or marginal one doesn't sour the price.
reviews.text: Quality control could be a little better, but the price for the multipack is tough to beat.

reviews.title: For the cost… I am super happy with them.
reviews.text: I’m giving these earbuds five stars because they exceed my expectations when they only cost seventy cents each. I find it hard to complain about these, especially considering the cost. The sound is quite adequate for such earbuds. I use these in a classroom setting for kids who either cannot afford earbuds or forget there is at home. They are individually packaged. Nice assortment of colors. Though they are being sold by a US company, I do not show where they are made. I can only assume they are made in China.

reviews.title: Great Sound Just Wish they had a Microphone
reviews.text: Fun colors and love this type of ear plug over the cone shaped style. Listening to music on my phone sounds great  but I wish they had a microphone so I could easily answer calls. Now I have to unplug the earphones when the phone starts to ring and then re-plug after I'm done on the call. I guess they are really designed for use on exclusive music players. The listing is confusing because it says "Bluetooth" which they don't have, but the wire is about 4ft. long so your music unit can easily be kept anywhere convenient. No volume control either so that has to be handled on your phone/music player.

The keychain is pretty. The clip to attach it is smaller than I anticipated and almost didn't fit my purse. It will protect the case from scratches, but that's hardly why I bought it- it's sparkly and pretty and does the job well enough.

reviews.title: OK Headphones
reviews.text: I think these headphones are more like 3.5 stars but I rounded up.  I have kind of a small head for an adult female, and still these headphones were too tight.  Usually I can wear headphones made for kids but not these.  These are definitely for smaller heads and small ears since the ear cups get painful after a while since they aren't big enough to fully cover my ears.  The sound is o.k., nothing to write home about, but for the price (as of this review these head phones cost $19.44) I think they are worth it.  Great for kids, not so good for adults, even if you are a pinhead.

reviews.title: High quality headphones
reviews.text: These are perfect for my 4th grade child to use at school. It is hard to find headphones with plug-ins and that aren't wireless. For my son, the ones with a plug are best to use his school issued ipad. These are sturdy and I'm hoping they last the entire school year.

reviews.title: Not really for large heads.
reviews.text: All the talk about "classroom" and "child's" in the Amazon listing really means "this is a small headset." It's not big enough for me. I have a large (7 3/4) head and this thing actually hurts my ears (presses on them too hard). If you need headphones for a child then this would work just fine. Not for me.

reviews.title: Good headphones for kids
reviews.text: These are perfect for my 6 yr old. They fit her great and don’t get so loud that they can damage her ears.

reviews.title: Phone does not ring in earbuds.
reviews.text: I would have given a 5-star review, but my S20 FE 5G rings out loud for everyone to hear rather than in the earbuds.  I purchased these earbuds to listen to music at the office.  When my phone rings, the volume of the ring matches the volume of the music, which would be fine, except that it's not ringing in the earbuds.  It rings out loud, which disturbs those around me.

reviews.title: Great for outside work
reviews.text:
Was wearing these when taking down Christmas decorations and listening to an audio book.

My neighbor thought it was ignoring him but I just didn’t hear him - these have good noise cancellation. Sound quality was great too.

reviews.title: Solid Value
reviews.text: Regular ear buds don't fit in my ears so I need the "wings." I am very happy with how they perform but they are not as "full range" as more expensive brands.

reviews.title: Good customer service
reviews.text: I had some issues with my first set and the customer service was awesome. I appreciate a company that will backup their products.

reviews.title: Love these
reviews.text: These are comfortable and easy to use. Good sound as well.

reviews.title: Good sound quality
reviews.text:
These are good quality but my left ear is uncomfortable. My ear must be a different shape. I was hoping the battery life would be better, but its ok. I use it to listen to books while i work. Take calls when needed.
Most bluetooth headsets have a delay or echo. These do not. It makes phone calls very clear.
These are the best sound quality of all but my Dewalt headsets.
The battery lasts about 5 to 6 hours with my streaming usage. Probably last much longer with just calls.
My left ear hurts after a couple hours, but ny right ear is fine for all day.
They charge quickly about an hour maybe a little more.
DON'T MISPLACE THE CHARGING CASE they will become worthless. They can't be charged without it.
And it seems if they power off by themselves they have to be reset in the case.
All that being said i probably would buy them again.

reviews.title: Rival the best out there!!!
reviews.text:
Just make sure your find the right buds to fit your ear or they can fall out. But once they're in....
Frequency response at 25 hertz!!! You won't miss a thing.

reviews.title: Difficult to connect. Oddly LOW VOLUME and INTERFERES with apps like VIBER and DUO. Easy to loose.
reviews.text:
Where do I start:::
Connecting, if you want to use it with multiple devices, you can just forget it (if connected to one device, they will not allow you to connect to another device until you actually delete the connection from the current device even if it's off, did not have this issue with Apple Pods).
SOUND LEVEL is oddly LOW. Seriously, even at max setting does not even come close to even cheap plug-in buds, so can't use them in any loud place. Noise canceling does not help with that.
If used with call apps like Viber or Duo you'll be missing every other word. Don't know how or why, but connection signal goes to crap while these are on. As soon as I switch to plug-in buds, everything works just fine.
TOO DAMN SMALL. Sure it's convenient for the ear, but trying to handle them like putting in or taking out, better be sure to do it somewhere safe in case you drop them (not outside for sure). It would be better if they had over the year clips or something.
COLLECTS all your EAR-WAX. Yes, these things go in so deep that it just gets absolutely gross (never had this problem with other buds and I clean my ears every day)
